paper.title_en: Hybrid stars among mass gap objects are excluded by twin stars at $1.4\,M_\odot$
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the question whether compact objects in the so called mass gap ($2.5 < M/M_\odot < 5.0$) can be neutron stars or hybrid stars. Using a generic hybrid star equation of state with a first-order deconfinement transition, we map the allowed parameter space in a Seidov-type diagram and confront it with modern mass--radius constraints. We find that mass-gap hybrid stars require an extremely early onset of deconfinement and very stiff quark matter. The Bayesian analysis, however, favors equations of state with deconfinement at typical neutron-star masses around $1.4\,M_\odot$ with mass-twin stars that, if confirmed, would rule out hybrid stars as candidates for observed mass-gap compact objects.

reasons: 未达到主题关键词门槛
